methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $626M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

data.narrative:
Poolside is an AI & Software company at Series B led by Jason Warner and Eiso Kant, having raised $626M+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 same-stage, same-sector private companies ranging from $500M (Photoroom) to $32.0B (Safe Superintelligence), supplemented by 1 public comparable (PLTR) and 3 additional private references.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $626M total capital raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $1.2B. Peer-median analysis across the 5-company comparable set produces a median valuation of $5.0B, though the range is wide ($500M to $32.0B), reflecting significant dispersion in the AI frontier-tech cohort.

The triangulated analysis spans $1.0B (low), $3.1B (median), and $5.8B (high). The median estimate of $3.1B reflects material tension between capital-deployed pricing and peer benchmarking. Key uncertainties include whether Poolside's technology, customer traction, or burn profile warrants positioning at the lower end near stage-progression ($1.2B) or toward peer median ($5.0B). The wide peer range itself signals that comparable-company analysis in early-stage AI carries substantial valuation dispersion absent near-term revenue scale or differentiated unit economics disclosure.
